appearance:
Lucien Marchand moves through Elysium like a statue that decided to breathe, his presence so still and focused that he seems to fade into the background until you notice the intensity of his attention. Ghouled in 1927 at the age of twenty-seven, he should appear to be in his early thirties after nearly seventy years of slow aging—but there's something timeless about him, as if the vitae has preserved not just his body but his essence. He's tall and lean, with the kind of build that speaks to years of physical labor—strong shoulders from working with stone, steady hands that have never known a tremor, and a posture so precise it borders on mechanical. His hair is short and blond, kept practical rather than fashionable, and his face carries the sharp, angular features of northern France—high cheekbones, a straight nose, and intense blue eyes that miss nothing.

What's most striking about Lucien, however, is the way he carries the evidence of his work like a second skin. His hands are perpetually dusted with fine white powder from marble, plaster, and bone—a residue that clings to his cuticles, lines the creases of his palms, and sometimes traces across his face when he absently brushes back his hair. He wears practical clothing that accommodates his work: sturdy trousers that can withstand stone dust, shirts with sleeves that can be rolled up, and a work apron that's seen decades of use. Everything is clean but worn, functional rather than fashionable, and he moves with the economical grace of someone who has spent a lifetime perfecting every motion.

But it's his stillness that makes him unsettling—the way he can stand motionless for hours, watching Étienne work or observing the Kindred who pass through Elysium, his eyes cataloging details that others miss. When he does move, it's with the fluid precision of someone who has learned to shape matter with absolute control, and when he speaks—which is rare, only when asked direct questions—his voice is quiet, almost reverent, as if every word is a prayer to the art he serves. In the candlelit halls of Elysium or the shadowed corners of Étienne's gallery, Lucien Marchand is a reminder that devotion can be its own form of beauty, and that sometimes the most powerful presence is the one that barely seems to exist at all.
biography: Originally a Parisian conservator in his late 20s, Lucien Marchand was ghouled by Étienne Duvalier in 1927 and has served as his sculptor's assistant and personal attendant for nearly 70 years. Tall, quiet, and ghost-pale with short blond hair and intense blue eyes, his hands are perpetually dusted with fine white powder from marble, plaster, and bone. He moves like a statue that decided to breathe, utterly devoted to the belief that Étienne's art is divine work and that his purpose is to assist a god in creation. He rarely speaks unless asked direct questions and sometimes carves tiny, unfinished statues that he hides around Elysium - uncommissioned works reflecting Kindred faces seen in passing. Étienne occasionally finds and corrects them in their silent collaboration. He embodies Étienne's obsession with form - shaping matter with absolute precision.
equipment: Sculptor's tools (chisels, mallets, files), fine white powder residue, sketchbook of unfinished works, work apron

merits_flaws.name: Ghoul
merits_flaws.description: Fed Toreador vitae regularly since 1927. Gains Potence 1 automatically, plus Celerity 2 and Auspex 1 taught by domitor. Ages approximately 1 year per decade.
